Pre-eclampsia‚ó¶ HT >20W with proteinuria, +/- renal or liver upset, neurological problems, haematological disturbance, IUGR2. Gestational Hypertension‚ó¶ HT alone >20W3. Chronic Hypertension‚ó¶ HT preconception or in early pregnancy ÔÇñessential (90%); secondary (10%)4. Pre-eclampsia superimposed on chronic HTÔÅΩ Early Vs Late diseaseÔÅΩ More than just a spectrum of severity‚ó¶ <34 wks‚ó¶ A primarily fetal disorder‚ó¶ Placental dysfunction, reduced placental volumeÔÇñ Incomplete trophoblast spiral artery invasion‚ó¶ IUGR, abnormal dopplers, perinatal hazard‚ó¶ Multiorgan dysfunction and maternal hazard‚ó¶ >34 wks‚ó¶ A primarily maternaldisorder‚ó¶ Secondary to maternal microvascular disease or reflects an underlying genetic disposition ‚ó¶ Normal placenta, larger placental volumeÔÇñNormal spiral artery behaviour‚ó¶ Normal dopplers, Normal birth weight‚ó¶ More favourable neonatal and maternal outcomesÔÅΩ What NORMALLY happens ....and IS NOT happening in PET?ÔÅΩ The Placental Bed‚ó¶ Replacement of the endothelium and media smooth muscle cells‚ó¶ Dilatation to wide ‚Äònoncontractile‚Äôtubes‚ó¶ Loss of vasomotor controlGenetic predispositionPositive FHx: RR=3 for development ofpre-eclampsiaÔÇ∑ No single maternal or fetal gene Genetic predisposition Immunological dysfunction ‚ÄòImmune rejection‚Äô of the genetically foreign fetal allograft. ÔÇ∑ Increased PET among primigravid, reduced length of sexual co-habitation, barrier contraception use, sperm and oocyte donation.
